title: Mafia II

notes: Game doesn't work out of the box. If you use the mentioned mafia2.exe it works fine on Proton. I installed additionally D9VK and it worked as well fine with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, except some minor graphical glitches with anti alias. I would rate it gold, if you wouldn't have to download an exe file.
title: Mafia II

notes: Game does not launch unless you use the mafia2.exe provided in other reports (or get your own from installing the game via lutris/wine - see https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/530#issuecomment-431513884 ). After replacing the executable, it runs flawlessly. I did not run into any issues, graphical or otherwise. Using steam-native and compiz-manjaro in Manjaro Xfce.
title: Mafia II

notes: Game doesn't work out of the box. If you use the mentioned mafia2.exe it works fine on Proton. I installed additionally D9VK including the new ACO Mesa compiler and it worked as well fine with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, except some minor graphical glitches with anti alias. I would rate it gold, if you wouldn't have to download an exe file.
title: Mafia II

notes: This game only works if you replace the .exe file (check the link on reviews). After this, runs great but with some glitches like sky flicking or textures. BUT i was trying to find a solution and adding PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% the game works like a charm. enjoy!

title: Mafia II

notes: Platinum rating after two hours of kicking it around, it runs perfectly "out of the box", ALT-TAB to linux desktop and back to fullscreen game is flawless, CPU and GPU throttle down while on desktop.  Technically, my test has nothing to do with Proton, game installed inside Lutris 4.13 wine prefix with system wine version: wine-stable 4.0.2~buster.  All I did was a standard Lutris wizard install, didn't change any options, no moving a new executable file or anything manual required, like previous posts had to do to get it working.
title: Mafia II

notes: Update after 10+ hours of play: downgraded from Platinum to Silver. Technically, my test has nothing to do with Proton, game installed inside Lutris 4.13 wine prefix with system wine version: wine-stable 4.0.2~buster. All I did was a standard Lutris wizard install, didn't change any options, no moving a new executable file or anything manual required, like previous posts had to do to get it working.  (As a matter of fact, the linked 3rd party executable does not even run at all for me - crashes before game finishes loading - since I'm using the Lutris runner of Wine Steam, maybe a Proton only thing?)  Nevertheless, downgrade reason is flickering sky while in car on the road (as many others get), and consistently crashes after ~1.5 hours of active play.  But it's not Bronze, because everything else is great, and it's reliable outside of that - no other issues.  I would just exit the game and restart it every hour or so - game runs great and I probably could have continued to completion just fine like that (but I rage quit, after a hard boss with a checkpoint too far back dragged me thru too many repeat trash pulls to try again - not my cup of tea for game challenge)
title: Mafia II

notes:
I needed a replacement for the original mafia2.exe. Took it from here: https://1drv.ms/u/s!AjAOLXh0xtmCgfAoztroDiFYfd3ooA
In addition, I did not have to register additional commands. Everything is very simple, replace mafia2.exe and play.
title: Mafia II

notes: GPU is atually a 380X. A solid bronze for this game. For a start you need to download a questionable EXE from the internet. For me and most others, the game does not start otherwise. Proton 4.2-9 gave me marginal better performance over 4.11-6. The game drops frames all over the place, even with graphics turned down, it does help but still a mess. Up and down from 60fps to 10fps like a yo-yo. Disable Esync and use Wine D3D11, which nearly eliminates frame drops on high settings. At this point I'd give it a Silver rating but there's more. The sky glitches black while roaming around and there are bits of the menus missing too. I haven't tried it long enough to see if it crashes a lot. It did crash when I first got the game running, so I imagine that it will again at some point. For all I know this could all be because of the questionable EXE. I shall try it in Lutris next.
title: Mafia II

notes: Between running this on my Xeon system and this one, I think I've determined the optimum way to currently play this game on Proton. The Xeon is a low-ish clocked 12 threaded system and suffers frame drops all the time. This game is just too old to use all the cores effectively. Reducing graphical settings helps out a lot, it must give your CPU less to do, giving you more headroom. So, the best way to play this on here is with solid single core performance; pure clock speed. I'm clocked at 4.9GHz on all cores. There is times seemingly at random the game maxes out one CPU core, that is when frame drops seem to happen. It isn't using more than one core to do the work. Now whether that is a fault of the game or something proton is doing I dopnt know. With D9VK enabled too, the frame drops on this system are 97% better. Not perfect. Noticed weird lighting halos around characters outside in the game. There is some menu things missing too. If it were able to run with just these tweaks, I'd give it a gold rating. You need to download a questionable EXE to get things working at all. If that is fixed in future I'll come back and rate it gold.
title: Mafia II
